articles.title: Turtlemint Fintech Solutions share price makes weak debut, lists at 11% discount

articles.title: Persistent Systems share price crashes 9% to hit a 52-week low; is it a stock to buy?
articles.summary: Persistent Systems shares opened at ₹4,500 against their previous close of ₹4,840.45 and plunged 9% to their one-year low of ₹4,404. The BSE Information Technology index declined 1% during the session.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Transrail Lighting share price jumps 4% after winning overseas orders worth ₹459 crore
articles.summary: Transrail Lighting share price has risen 9% in one month and has gained 10% in three months. The smallcap stock has fallen 8% in six months, while it has dropped nearly 21% in one year.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.summary: Indian benchmark indices opened mixed following a significant weekly winning streak. The Nifty 50 showed little change, while the BSE Sensex dipped slightly. Investors are cautious amid geopolitical tensions, with attention on US-Iran diplomatic efforts.

articles.title: Gold rate declines on MCX as fresh escalations in US-Iran tensions drive oil prices up, fueling US Fed rate-hike bets
articles.summary: Gold and silver rates declined on MCX as fresh escalations in US-Iran tensions drove oil prices up. MCX gold August futures were 0.40% down at ₹1,43,583 per 10 grams, while MCX silver September contracts were 0.26% down at ₹2,22,899 per kg around 9:15 am.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Rupee opens 5 paise higher at 94.35 against US dollar
articles.summary: The Indian rupee strengthened to 94.35 against the US dollar, supported by lower oil prices despite potential global risks. Analysts suggest the rupee may trade within the 94-95 range, with strong capital inflows providing some buffer against a firm US dollar.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Vijay Kedia explains why every bull market ends the same way—and where the next opportunity could be
articles.summary: Vijay Kedia shares insights on market cycles, explaining that investment opportunities rotate through phases of optimism, euphoria, and correction. He encourages investors to recognize patterns in asset classes and advises focusing on long-term strategies rather than short-term trends.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: India regains top spot in EM tracker, but higher cost pressures continue to linger
articles.summary: Stronger exports and factory activity helped India reclaim the top spot in Mint's Emerging Markets Tracker. However, higher crude prices during the West Asia war have raised wholesale inflation and input costs, delaying relief for businesses despite the recent easing in oil prices.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: North-East floods raise power, logistics and inflation risks
articles.summary: The disruptions could have wider consequences for the rest of the country, where power demand remains high amid persistent heat and an uneven monsoon.

articles.title: Modi spotlights shift to public transport, natural fertilizers amid Gulf crisis
articles.summary: Addressing the nation in his monthly radio broadcast ‘Mann Ki Baat’, Modi said that following his appeal to avoid foreign travel and cut gold purchases, many people have postponed overseas holidays and chosen not to buy new gold, with some instead recycling old gold to make new jewellery.

articles.title: Govt eases Quality Control Order rules: Which industry segments are likely to get relief?
articles.summary: The government's new transition framework offers relief to manufacturers in sectors such as toys, personal protective equipment (PPE), air conditioners, footwear, furniture and select electrical appliances.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: AI job apocalypse ‘unlikely,' could create new opportunities over time: Goldman Sachs report
articles.summary: A Goldman Sachs report says fears of an AI-driven jobs apocalypse are exaggerated, despite significant labour market disruption ahead. Experts expect AI to displace some workers while creating new jobs over time.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Iranian oil may return, but Indian refiners eye concessions
articles.summary: As Indian refiners await clarity in terms of payment mechanism and long-term durability of the US-Iran peace talks, they may not rush for immediate procurement of Iranian crude and are taking a more long-term approach, to ensure supplies when the sanctions are permanently waived off
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: For better outcomes, Centre nudges states on focused capex for ₹2 tn loan scheme
articles.summary: The DoE has asked states and UTs to limit their proposals under its SASCI scheme for capex to their choice of maximum five priority sectors in FY27. The move marks a shift from simply encouraging higher capex to ensuring more concentrated investments that deliver measurable development outcomes.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Global giants backing India’s shipbuilding boom, says shipping secretary
articles.summary: Driven by a ₹70,000-crore policy package, domestic yards have secured huge export orders while the government backs five massive manufacturing clusters to scale up capacity, Vijay Kumar, secretary at the shipping ministry, told Mint in an exclusive interview.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Businesses embrace GST, but seek more reforms: Deloitte survey
articles.summary: The findings come at a time when GST collections continue to be robust. In May, the Centre and states together collected ₹1.94 trillion in gross GST revenue, before adjusting for refunds, up 3.2% from ₹1.88 trillion mopped up a year ago.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Why India’s consumers may stay cautious despite easing oil prices
articles.summary: Falling crude oil prices offer hope, but delayed rains, food inflation and higher living costs continue to weaken consumer confidence.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Millets, check dams and emergency plans: How India is preparing for dry days
articles.summary: Low monsoon rainfall is significant, as the season accounts for about 70% of India's annual rainfall. The rural economy, which relies heavily on agriculture, remains an important driver of economic growth.

articles.summary: India and the US are moving closer to finalizing a bilateral trade agreement, with commerce minister Piyush Goyal and USTR Jamieson Greer holding talks in New Delhi. Negotiations focus on tariffs, market access and digital trade ahead of a key US tariff deadline in July.

articles.title: India's private sector output moderates further in June
articles.summary: June saw slower growth in new orders, international sales, employment and business activity.
